Dance Central - Running Finish
Quickstep Running Finish
The Quickstep Running Finish is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DW | T | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side and slightly fwd | Pointing to LOD | T | 3/8 between 1-2, body turns less | Sway(L) | Up
Man can turn 1/4 to end DW on new LOD. 
Man should shape left to allow lady to pass and to make the turn easier.
Q: LF fwd preparing to step OP left side leading | Facing LOD | TH | Body turn to R | Sway(L) | Up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing LOD | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM 
Follower
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Really drive the step, go through Man
Q: LF to side | Backing DC | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | -- | Up
L side is very stretched, lift L hip
Can shape to R, head goes with shape, to R
Lady can turn 1/8, will end BDW on new LOD
Q: RF back, R side leading | Backing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | -- | Up, lower e/o 3
Lower and push off standing R leg, bigger next step.
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing LOD | T | Sway(S) 
Notes
Timing can also be: QQSS
Can be commenced in other alignments, e.g. starting BLOD, ending DC.
Man may turn 1/4 between 1-2 when a facing alignment would be used. Lady would turn 1/8 between 1-2 and 1/8 between 2-3.
Preceding Figures
Back Lock
Cross Swivel
Hover Corte
Natural Turn And Back Lock
Progressive Chasse To Right
Following Figures
Forward Lock
Natural Turn And Back Lock
Natural Pivot Turn
Natural Spin Turn
Natural Turn With Hesitation
Natural Turn
Quick Open Reverse
Fish Tail
Four Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Four Quick Run; or at corner, LF back with lady OP, TH, S into Four Quick Runs)
V6 (2-12, at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into 2-12 V6; or 2-12, at corner, LF back with lady OP, TH, S into Four Quick Runs)
Tipsy To L (then into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD.; or then into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD.)
Six Quick Run (at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Six Quick Run; or at corner, LF back with Lady OP, TH, S into Six Quick Run.)

Dance Central - Cross Swivel
QuickStep Cross Swivel
The QuickStep Cross Swivel is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | H | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | --
S: RF closes (or nearly closes) to LF slightly back w/o weight | Facing DC | pressure on T of LF with foot flat and pressure on IE of T of RF | 1/4 to L between 1-2 | Sway(L) | --
Alternatively for styling, RF to side, knees inward
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: RF back | BDW | TH | start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM 
Use hip joint to start the turn, standing knee (R knee) stays forward over the standing foot (RF), do not move the knee off the standing foot. 
Use ab muscle to pull to the back of foot, do not leave middle fwd and shoulder weight back.
Heel of RF does not lower until the turn is completed. 
Head is still left. At end of &: there is "up" rise feeling before the next S lower to normal.
S: LF closes to RF slightly fwd w/o weight | Backing DC | H then IE of T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | --
Alternatively for styling, LF to side, knee inward, a little space is ok.
Body forms a line, slanted straight to R, keep left side lifted. Give R side to Man. Stay connected.
Turn head to R.
At end of &: rise in body before next S to lower to normal. (not "sinking down").
S: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM
Arms "breathe", give arms to R. 
Preceding Figures
Double Reverse Spin
Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left)
Reverse Pivot
Following Figures
Tipple Chasse To Right
Running Finish
Fish Tail
Tipsy To R (After LF back with Lady OP, TH, S, into Tipsy to R)

Dance Central - Hover Telemark to PP
Foxtrot Hover Telemark to Promenade Position
The Hover Telemark to Promenade Position is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing DW | HT | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side LF brushes towards RF | Facing LOD | T (RF), then IE of T of LF | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Continue to rise on 2
Q: LF diag fwd L side leading in PP | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | TH | 1/8 between 2-3. body turns less | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in PP and CBMP | Pointing DC, body facing LOD | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note unusual type of rise
Follower
S: RF back | Backing DW | TH | Start to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise e/o 1 NFR
RF straight back.
Q: LF to side, RF brushes toward LF | Backing LOD | T (LF) then IE of T of RF | 1/8 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Continue to rise on 2
LF is more of the back step, than side step. 
Q: RF to side in PP | Pointing to center (moving DC) | TH | 1/4 to R, body turns less | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 3
S: LF fwd and across in PP and CBMP | Pointing to center (moving DC) | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Following Figures
Natural Weave (overturned, commencing in PP)
Feather Step
Weave from PP
Natural Zig-Zag from PP
Curved Feather to Back Feather (at corner, starting in PP)

Dance Central - Cross Chasse
Quickstep Cross Chasse
The Quickstep Cross Chasse is a Bronze Level figure, listed as an additional step that can be danced in Medal Tests. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd, OP | FDW | H 
Man steps OP.
S: LF fwd | FDW | HT | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF to side | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF closes to RF | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
At the end of this, the body is wound up perfect for leading natural turning figures (with first step CBMP , OP)
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Continue into Forward Lock in Outside Partner Position. Use CBM as you take the step.
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH 
Lengthen R side.
S: RF back | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR
Lady connects R should down.
Q: LF to side | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Maintain the shape, push on RF/thigh. 
Styling option: turn head to R
Q: RF closes to LF | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Running Cross Chasse
In stead of closing feet above, keep moving ('running") forward:
Leader
S: RF fwd, CBMP, OP | FDW | HT 
S: LF fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: RF diagonally fwd | FDW | T | -- | Sway(L) | up
Q: LF fwd, L shoulder leading | FDW | TH | -- | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | FDW | H | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back, CBMP | BDW | TH 
Pay attention not to do a lot rotation through shoulders.
S: RF back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1, NFR 
R shoulder connects down. 
Q: LF diagonally back | BDW | T | -- | Sway(R) | up
Do not rotate L shoulder.
Stretch L side.
Q: RF back, R shoulder leading | BDW | TH | -- | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
S: LF back in CBMP | BDW | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Preceding Figures
Change of Direction
Double Reverse Spin
Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left)
Natural Turn
Reverse Pivot
Following Figures
Natural Turn And Back Lock
Natural Pivot Turn
Natural Spin Turn
Natural Turn With Hesitation
Natural Turn

Dance Central - Natural Hover Telemark
Foxtrot Natural Hover Telemark
The Foxtrot Hover Telemark is a Gold Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing DW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1
Q: LF to side | Backing DC | TH | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up. Lower e/o 2
Q: RF to side (Heel Pull) | Facing DC | H, IE of foot, whole foot | 1/2 between 2-3 | Sway(L) | Down on 3. Rise e/o 3
S: L knee veers inwards | Facing DC | T (RF) with pressure on IE of T of LF | Slight body turn to R | Sway(L) | Up.
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing DC | T | - | Sway(L) | Up.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | - | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 6.
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | -
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | Rise e/o 1. NFR
L shoulder connects down.
Body should feel fwd, don't turn shoulder back.
Diagonal stretch through body upward rightward up.
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | Up. Lower e/o 2. NFR
Q: LF to side | Wall | T of LF | 3/8 between 2-3 | Sway(R) | Down on 3. Rise e/o 3
LF is really fwd step and foot ending up as a side step.
Cushion the knee. Pull stomach muscle.
Don't push tummy toward partner to try to connect.
S: RF brushes to LF | BDC | IE of Toe of RF | Slight body turn to R | Sway(R) | Up.
Body shape goes to the R (can take head to R as well, or keep head L)
Q: RF diag back R side leading | BDC | TH | - | Sway(R) | Up.
Feather step finish. Swing rib cage to R. Dancing head to L. Changing shape to L. 
Q: LF back in CBMP | BDC | TH | - | Sway(S) | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 6.
S: RF back | BDC | TH | - | Sway(S) | CBM | - 
Preceding Figures
Closed Telemark
Hover Telemark
Three Step
Following Figures
Reverse Turn
Closed Telemark
Open Telemark and Feather Ending
Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending

Dance Central - Running Right Turn
QuickStep Running Right Turn
The QuickStep Running Right Turn is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Think of Running Right Turn as a couple of figures strung together: Natural Pivot, Half Natural Turn (Heel Turn for Lady), Running Finish (OP)
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Step straight forward, do not veer to right, 
Q: LF side | BDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | up
Q: RF closes to LF | BLOD, down LOD, toe turned in | TH | 1/8 to R between 2-3 | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 3
Settle at end.
S: LF back | FLOD | THT | 1/2 to R on 4 (pivot) | Sway(S) | CBM | --
(Pivot): do not lean forward and stick butt out.
S: RF fwd in CBMP | facing LOD | HT |continue to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 5
Man step straight fwd
S: LF to side | BDC | T | 3/8 btwn 5-6 | Sway(R) | up
Man does not have to complete turn with his body on this step, the turn completes through next step
S: RF back R side leading | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 6-7 | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 7
Body completes turn
Q: LF back in CBMP | BLOD | T | continue to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 8
Running fish.
Q: RF to side and slightly fwd | Facing new LOD | T | 1/4 btwn 8-9 | Sway(L) | up
S: LF fwd, preparing to step OP L side leading | LOD | TH | - | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 10
S: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | LOD | H | - | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
Don't be "steppy" - all steps are smooth flowing.
Slightly to side to give Man room to step. Use CBM to open door for Man to pass.
Q: RF to side | Pointing LOD | T | 3/8 to R between 1-2, body turns less | Sway(L) | up
R leg track Man's R leg. Otherwise Lady will gap with Man.
Extend head to L.
Q: LF closes to RF | Facing LOD | TH | body completes turn | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 3
Feet together, settle and prep legs. Do not break at hip or stick butt out. 
S: RF fwd | LOD | HTH | 1/2 to R on 4 (pivot) | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Pivot on RF.
Lady can keep head strongly to the left; be careful not to bring head into Man's space.
Or, Lady can turn her head to right (which, when done right is preferable, will help Man with the pivot.). 
Head should stay on Man's right side, do not let head get in front of Man if turning head to right.
When shaping head to right, it's both a slight "dip" to right, as well as rotating to the right at the same time.
Keep L leg where it is, do not close up with R leg after pivot, need to leave room for Man. Man is tracking the leg.
During the pivot and heel turn, keep right side "positive" (in front of body, stretched)
S: LF back | BLOD | TH | continue to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 5, NFR
L leg should have been been left where it was during pivot, just step back.
Curve.
S: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | facing LOD | HT | 1/2 btwn 5-6 | Sway(L) | up
Heel turn. Draw in R heel slowly, R side is slightly raised for a quick moment. (Man lifting side for heel turn.) Do not close both feet too fast.
Leave head, head stretched very left, send middle forward to Man, turn body, so head is stretched to left even more. hip more forward. which makes head extension bigger. Then stay connected with Man. 
S: LF fwd preparing to step OP, L side leading | facing LOD | TH | - | Sway(L) | up, lower e/o 7
At beginning of this step, R side is still positive till getting ready for next step (RF OP).
Rise on toes and sending middle part of body and left side strongly forward and around Man (to keep connection with Man). LF forward, push from standing R leg. Keep body rotating, very twisted to R, head stretched. 
It appears to have big extension, when in fact it's just sending middle body forward. L toe keep pressure on floor to keep balance.
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | facing LOD | HT | starts to turn | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 8
This timing is QQS. Running Finish is also often danced as SQQ.
SQQ: Running Finish, head turned to R. Don't get too close to Man, body to Man, but extend to R. Ending weight on RF, get into R knee.
RF strong forward step. Keep left side up and around Man. Do not break at the hip. Make sure the whole R side including middle of body is still connected with Man, then stretch as much as possible.
Styling option: head turn to right, but body still needs to stay to left, only turn head
Q: LF to side | BDC of new LOD | T | 1/8 btwn 8-9 | Sway(R) | up
Point LF forward, big step, swing L leg out ahead of body, then swivel LF (it becomes a side step). 
Styling option: turn head to R (if shaping to R, commit to it, don't do it half way.)
S: RF back, R side leading | BLOD | TH | 1/8 btwn 9-10 | Sway(R) | up, lower e/o 10
Ending weight on RF, get into R knee. Don't fall back to next step.
Keep head distance from Man, don't close up head space with Man, keep frontal curve back. Stay left.
At end of the step, R side is positive.
S: LF back in CBMP | BLOD | T | - | Sway(S) | CBM | --
e.g followed by a Natural Turn. If head had turned to right earlier in the Running Finish, keep head there, turn body to right, which will return head to normal left position. Keep it smooth.
Notes:
Other alignments:
Across corner: 3/8 on Pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish.
Two corners: 3/8 on Pivot, 3/8 on Natural Turn, 1/4 on Running Finish
Side of room: 1/2 on pivot, 1/2 on Natural Turn, 3/8 on Running Finish
When 3/8 is made on Running Finish, using "pointing" instead of "facing" at step 9 for Man.
Alternative timing SQQ may be used on steps 5-7, in which case, SQQS will be used on the Running Finish.
Preceding Figures
Closed Telemark
Fish Tail
Four Quick Run
Progressive Chasse
Rumba Cross
Running Right Turn
V6
Following Figures
Running Right Turn
Fish Tail (normal; or when ended DC)
Natural Turn And Back Lock
Natural Pivot Turn
Natural Spin Turn
Natural Turn With Hesitation
Natural Turn
Quarter Turn to Right
Forward Lock
Quick Open Reverse (when ended DC)
Tipsy To L (Tipsy to L into 2-12 V6 DC or 2-5 Back Lock down LOD)

Dance Central - Quarter Turn to Right
This could start in closed position or outside partner position, depending on the previous step.
QuickStep Quarter Turn to Right
The QuickStep Quarter Turn to Right is Newcomer Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Starting direction: DW or LOD (depending on previous figure)
Leader
S: RF fwd | FDW | HT | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1
Roll your foot, so it's not jerky.
When starting in closed position: step in between follower's legs.
When starting in outside partner position: step RF outside follower's R leg.
Q: LF side | BC | T | 1/8 to R between 1-2 | Sway(R) | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Q: RF closes to LF | BDC | T | 1/8 to R between 2-3 | Sway(R) | -- | No rise & fall
S: LF side/slightly back | BDC | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 4
Don't let head and shoulder weight drop back. It's controlled action.
Follower
S: LF back | BDW | TH | Start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | start to rise e/o 1, NFR
When starting a dance, this is not OP.
After initial figure, this will always be OP.
Q: RF side | PDC | T | 1/4 to R between 1-2, body turns less | Sway(L) | cont to rise on 2 and 3
Keep head to left, do not allow head and shoulder to start going to the right
In Quarter Turn, the turn happens sooner (compared to heel turns), swing R leg/hip to side. Compared to Natural Turn, this is less effort (less turn). 
Q: LF close to RF | FDC | T | slight body turn | Sway(L) | -- | No rise & fall
In Quarter Turn, there is a check point at end of step 3 (not like Chasse which progressive, trapezoid movement). 
S: RF diagonal fwd | FDC | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up, lower e/o 4
Finish square, inline. Step in between Man's legs.
Note:
If this figure starts facing LOD:
Leader: turn 1/4 to R between 1-2, and 1/8 between 2-3.
Follower: turn 3/8 to R between 1-2, body turns less, finish the turn on 3.
Preceding Figures
Closed Telemark
Fish Tail
Four Quick Run
Progressive Chasse
Running Right Turn
V6
Following Figures
Progressive Chasse
Reverse Pivot
Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left)
Four Quick Run

Hover Corte

Dance Central - Natural Telemark
Foxtrot Natural Telemark
The Natural Telemark is a Silver Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Leader
S: RF fwd | Facing DW | HT | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise e/o 1
Leading heel turn, stretch body through diagonal. The sway is less than other figures. There is more rotation in this figure.
Q: LF to side | Backing DC | T | 1/4 between 1-2 | Sway(R) | Up
Continue turning through the feet.
Q: RF side (small step) | Facing DC | T | 1/2 between 2-3. | Sway(S) | Up
Compress into knee without lowering into heel.
Continue through diagonal.
Q: LF diag fwd, preparing to step OP, L side leading | Facing DC | T | -- | Sway(L) | Up
Q: RF fwd in CBMP, OP | Facing DC | TH | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 5
S: LF fwd | Facing DC | H | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note -- 1/2 or 5/8 turn may be used at a corner. Amount of turn in each case will be 1/4 between 1/2, whether started facing LOD or DW
Follower
S: LF back | Backing DW | TH | start to turn R | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Heel Turn to Right, access point is through left shoulder. Body has strong diagonal, feel body is going forward, don't think about bending upper body backward.
Q: RF closes to LF (Heel Turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 3/8 between 1-2 | Sway(L) | continue to rise on 2
Q: LF to side, RF brushes toward LF | Backing DC | T | 3/8 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Take head and weight to LF, compress L knee, then brush RF.
This step stays on T, there is no toe release for LF into next step.
Q: RF diag back R side leading | Backing DC | TH | -- | Sway(R) | Up
Bring LF underneath body, skimming floor, then step back.
Q: LF back in CBMP | Backing DC | TH | -- | Sway(S) | Up. NFR. Lower e/o 5
There is RF toe release when LF is stepping back on this step.
S: RF back | Backing DC | T | -- | Sway(S) | CBM | --
Note -- When underturned, the Lady will always turn 3/8 between 1-2
Preceding Figures
Closed Telemark
Hover Telemark
Three Step
Following Figures
Reverse Turn
Closed Telemark
Open Telemark and Feather Ending
Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending
Three Step (at corner)
Change Of Direction (at corner)
Hover Telemark (at corner)
Reverse Wave (at corner)
Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot

Dance Central - Double Reverse Spin
Quickstep Double Reverse Spin
The Quickstep Double Reverse Spin is a Bronze Level figure. This online reference gives a detailed description of the dance steps, including timing, footwork, alignment, and movement; steps diagram; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
The technical details are the same as in Waltz Double Reverse Spin.
Leader
S: LF fwd | Facing LOD | HT | Stars to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | Start to rise at end of 1
Do not collapse left side. Stretch left side and not lean toward inside of the turn.
Rise to toe - early rise.
S: RF to side | BDW | T | 3/8 btwn 1-2 | Sway(S) | Up
Wait until the lady's feet are together before taking this step.
S: LF closes to RF without weight (Toe Pivot) | Facing DW | T | 1/2 btwn 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up, Lower at end of 3
Follower
S: RF back | BLOD | TH | starts to turn L | Sway(S) | CBM | rise slightly e/o 1, NFR
Very important to do CBM, with CBM shoulder will clear way for Man to pass. keep shoulder flat, and do not drop it on one side.
Use head weight back.
RF bigger step back.
Make sure keep body and head very left to Man, keep left side lifted. Keep the volume of the frame. Do no allow shoulder to rotate too quickly pass the hips, which will pull the Man into Lady's space and making hips rotate away from partner.
Do not take elbow back. Think about giving the arm to Man, keeping it forward throughout the figure. Take R shoulder onto RF, not pass RF.
S: LF closes to RF (Heel turn) | Facing LOD | HT | 1/2 between 1 and 2 | Sway(S) | Cont to rise on 2
Draws trailing foot in slowly, both legs should straighten,
Turn head slightly more to left (don't do it too early, though)
Draw center back to spine. Do not leave body to Man.
Q: RF to side and slightly back | Backing wall | T | 1/4 between 2-3 | Sway(S) | Up
Bigger step fwd, stay with Man, keep on his R side. Slight swing.
Make this fluid with previous heel turn and use LF to push off, then swivel on RF. Like in Viennese Waltz.
Keeping arms forward, give arms to Man.
Q: LF crosses in front of RF | BDW | TH | 1/8 between 3-4 | Sway(S) | Up. Lower e/o 4
Do not lower too early, lower on "&"
Finish with L side positive/fwd.
Note:
The Double Reverse Spin may be started LOD (chart above), DC, or DW.
When starting DW, lady: before stepping RF back, turn knee 1/8 to L, step back, so RF is stepping straight down LOD. Body feel quite twisted.
The step 1 of both Double Reverse Spin and Progressive Chasse to Right feel the same, the difference is the next step.
For Double Reverse Spin: Man steps DC, toward Lady's left shoulder (not her sternum).
For Progressive Chasse to Right, Man moves toward LOD, making Lady to step pass her LF to the side.
Double Reverse Spin is a traveling step, first Man goes, then Lady goes. It needs to move down the floor.
The amount of turn may vary between 3/4 and a whole turn.
Complete Turn:
step 1-2: Man 3/8, Lady: 1/2
step 2-3: Man 5/8: Lady: 3/8
step 3-4: lady: Lady: 1/8
3/4 Turn:
step 1-2: Man 1/4 (or 3/8), Lady: 3/8
step 2-3: Man: 1/2 (or 3/8), Lady: 1/4

Strong range of motion, curving body to inside of the turn.

Follower
S: RF fwd, pivot | LOD | HT | 1/2 pivot turn
SQQ: "Spin Turn" part
Get shoulder weight connecting down to foot (previous step), using the standing leg to push. then pivot on RF. 
Do not use shoulders to help with the turn. Power comes from the leg.
S: LF back, slightly leftwards | BLOD | T | 3/8 to R
Use the ball of LF. Flex into the knee. swivel on LF. 
Q: RF fwd, slightly rightwards | DC | T
Relax R shoulder down to RF, R knee is bent. then drive next step.
The strongest side, the driving side is the lowest point , not the swinging side. 
Q: LF fwd, L side leading | DC | T

Follower

Primary contact between man and lady is the inside of the lady's upper thighs: her right thigh to inside of his R leg; her left thigh to outside of his R leg.
Lady's L leg tracks Man's R leg when stepping this step.
LF steps in between Man's legs (Man is stepping his RF back and is turning, which makes room for Lady to step in between his feet), straight forward, do not veer to side.

Keep the volume in frame, don't make frame smaller. extend arm.
Notes:
If the Reverse Pivot is started backing DC the alignment for the Pivot (Man) would be "to center, toe turned in". 
Note also that this step is in CBMP owing to the body turning strongly to L as the RF foot slips back.

The Weave from PP could follow step 7 in which case the aligment of the Weave from PP would be as in the Waltz Weave from PP.

When turning left, the right side is the anchor point, left side stretch. 
First part of turn is done through feet (base), feet turn more. Do not bring shoulders back too quickly.
Head is still L. Head slowly turns between end of this step and next Q. Don't open head too early, arrive on L leg first, wait for Man's lead to open head. Turn head slowly "milk it".

Allow Man to get ahead of you. 
Left shoulder connects to left hip. Keep shaping to left.
Allow arms to swing across the body.
Then dance straight forward, follow the Man with upper body, legs are tracking straight.

Head position changes to left. Leave head behind, send middle of body forward, finish head with more extension. Keeping the wound-up shape for next step.
Movement is smooth and continuous, no stopping. 
One style is to keep slight R side lead through the Passing Natural.

Shoulder should end parallel with Man with hips turned out more.
Hips and and feet are in same alignment.

Rumba Spot Turns and Switch Turns
These are Newcomer Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
This is similar to Cha Cha Cha Spot Turns (including Switch Turns, Underarm Turns) (replacing chasse with one step).
Leader
2: RF fwd (after turning 1/4 to L on preceding "&") in line with LF. Then turn 1/2 to end with RF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to L, step RF, then 1/2 to L
This is Switch Turn to Left.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to LF
"3&", on "&": turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
At end of "1": turn to R.
2: LF fwd (after 1/4 to R) in line with RF, then turn 1/2 to end with LF in the back | 1/4 to R, step LF, then 1/2 to R
This is Switch Turn to Right.
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
3: Transfer weight to RF
on "&", turn 1/4 to R
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Follower
2: LF fwd (after turning 1/4 to R on preceding "&") in line with RF, Then turn 1/2 to R to end with LF in the back on "&" | 1/4 to R, step LF, Then 1/2 to R on "&"
This is Switch Turn to Left (because Man is turning L)
End of preceding step on '&', full commit the weight on RF, settle, pressure on LF, energy is forward, tail bone down; then swivel on RF, do quick impulse, turn 1/4 to R. Pay attention to foot position, LF turned out, not turned in (sometimes due to over turning to R on this step.)
Different from New Yorker: Fully commit weight on front foot before doing the half turn to R using Forward Walk Turn action.. (Difference in lead compared to New Yorker.)
Arm option: left hand on waist, or left arm wrap around the back. R arm can be held in front of tummy, palm facing tummy. Hands should not have other extra unintended actions, go directly to waist, pure and simple (if that's the choice).
On "&" after the turn, compress on left side. (Finishing with hips to the side, then, quick impulse, it will twist to move spine to RF in next step.)
To achieve the effect of "quick impulse" - sudden action:
body turns more continuously at same speed,
head: do head spotting and fast changing head direction
leg/knee delays the turn a bit and then turn much faster and body and leg arrives at the final position at the same time.
3: Transfer weight on to RF
Options:
before "3", there is a moment the R leg is bent, then straighten before stepping on floor.
or: Keep R leg straight the whole time.
Hold this a bit longer and have weight fully transfer to RF (see Latin Technique Swivel Action) and make sure to finish the diagonal pendulum swing before doing twist on '&'.
On "&", quick impulse, twist, turn 1/4 to R (turn before stepping next step)
When turning to right, it's Right Side leading.
Styling: when moving forward, arms would go back (think of wind blowing at the arms.)
4 1: LF to side | 1/4 to R over 3-5
Turn/swivel at end of previous beat. Track LF underneath and then place under the shoulder to the side, not too big step. It's a smooth step, do not pause in middle. "4" is a side step (after the turning to face partner), do not make it a forward step, then turn. 
At end of this step, work RF down to ground, develop the foot more.
For underarm turn, at the "&": twist L hip, pulling R hip through, R arm will close in front of face (to left side). Keep L shoulder fwd side leading.
2: RF fwd (after 1/4 to L) in line with LF, Then turn 1/2 to L ending with RF in the back | 1/4 to L, then 1/2 to L
Switch Turn to Right (Man is turning to R)
The turn is started at the end of preceding figure.
When turning at the end of "2", body is not square, use Left Side Leading.
If Lady's R arm is connected to Man, Lady will feel R arm closing window.
Static body design: hands statically on hips while turning and extend arms to side on 41.
More flexible and fluid design: When turning, L arm extend to the side and as body rotate, arm will warp in front of tummy, R hand on hip or R arm wrap around the back.
For Switch Turns, if Man is still hold Lady's R hand, Lady: Put LH on hip first, (end of previous beat) then raising L arm to side during "2", at beginning of count "3", L arm goes in front of chest. (and Man will release hold to Lady's RH, Lady's R arm wrap around back.)
3: Transfer weight to LF 
on "&", turn 1/4 to L
4 1: RF to side | 1/4 to L over 3-5
When followed by underarm turn or Alemana, L arm will be straight out to the side.
Note:
Spot Turns: 3 forward steps danced solo by Man and/or Lady, circling to L or R.
Spot Turns can be danced as Switch Turns. Start in Closed Position. No hold, or L to R or R to L hand hold.
Turning action: could be a Forward Walk Turn action, or a brushing action where the free foot closes to standing foot, brush and then do a fwd walk.
Different from underarm turn, for spot turns,
count "2": lady turns about 1/8", diagonally "&" RF brush LF
count "3": lady turns about 1/4, RF fwd
if preferred, the ball of the foot may remain in place while turn is made around this foot.
count "4 1": finish turn to face partner
last step could be fwd or to side depending on distance between couple.
Often, one partner is doing Spot Turn, while the other does Alternative Basic or Cuban Rock, or 3 steps of a Closed Basic.
Spot Turn is a common ending for New York, Hand to Hand, Fencing and may other figures.
Amount of Turns: complete turn, or 7/8 or 3/4, depending on starting and ending position: open PP, Open CPP, facing partner.
Spot Turn or Switch to L (Lady to R) could end in Open Position, taking L to R hand hold at step 3, taking step 3 fwd leading Lady to step back.

Rope Spinning is commenced during the last step of the preceding figure. Man leads Lady to turn sharply R under raised L arm, inclining and turning his body slightly R, releasing R hand hold. Side Step may be curved to L or R.

Dance Central - Basic Movements
These are Newcomer Level figures. This online reference gives a detailed description of dance steps, including timing, and movement; a list of figures that may precede or follow the pattern; and videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.
Rumba Closed Basic Movements
Closed Position. 
Turn: No Turn, 1/8 to 1/4 turn are allowed. 1/8 Turn is described below.
Leader
2: LF fwd
Body lift up, Check/settle on "a". Check before switching direction, as late as possible.
3: Transfer weight on RF | 1/8 to L over 2-3
On "a", collect LF to closer to RF before stepping to side.
4 1: LF to side and slightly back
Hold on 4 1, rotating hips, on "a", collect RF, step back.
2: RF back
3: Transfer weight to LF | 1/8 to L over 5-6
4 1: RF to side 
Follower
2: RF back
See Latin Technique and Rumba Technique for more details.
"Out"- Separate with partner. settle more, but make sure to have forward tone to Man, not pulling Man toward you.
Squeeze/compress the side of the standing leg. (R leg at for this.)
Twist right side to cause LF to move forward, pendulum hip action diagonally.
3: Transfer weight on LF | 1/8 to L over 2-3
Continue pendulum action forward.
"In" - close up with Man, really show weight transfer to front of the foot, L hip high (really make sure to dance this fully), Squeeze L side, then twist, simultaneously RF to side.
4 1: RF to side 
Some coaches say this would be "side and slightly forward, not directly to the side".
Feel the energy in upper body.
Hip pendulum diagonally forward to R. Squeeze R side.
Transfer weight to RF. Twist right side to cause LF fwd.
2: LF fwd
Sharp, quick foot movement on previous "&". This is Forward Checked Walk.
Hip pendulum diagonally forward to left. Split weight with flat foot, then
On &, allow hip to "finish" (Finish all the way with spine over the foot), then twisting to neutral position. Twisting causes R leg knee checking action, with R knee goes to the spot. RF 90 degree arch.
3: Transfer weight to RF | 1/8 to L over 5-6
RF ball flat.
Hip is laterally to right. Then twist to move LF to side
4 1: LF to side and slightly back
R side twist to move L leg to side. L hip will be pendulum diagonally fwd (yes, fwd)
Compress into left side, at the same time, RF start moving back (continuing with closed basic.)
Note:
One should feel a continuous figure 8 action all the time.
Closed Basic Movement may end with taking step 6 in Open CPP, Man turning 1/8 R, Lady 1/8 L, Man release R hand hold.
Silver & Gold: Turn 3/8 to L over 4-6 to get to Contact Position.
step 3: RF side and slightly fwd. Lady LF behind RF as Cuban Cross.
Follow with 4-6 or 4-9 of Reverse Top.

Basic In Place
3 steps are danced in place with feet together.
Closed Position, No Turn.

Alternative Basic
Closed Position, start with feet apart.
Normal hold, L to R hand hold, no hold.
Think of walking "--" horizontal line.
This can be danced in place of Closed Basic Movements.
LF Alternative Basic Movement may end in Open PP, Man turning 1/8 L and Lady 1/8 R on 3. Likewise, RF Alternative Basic may end in Open CPP.
Leader
2: LF closes to RF
This is LF Alternative Basic Movement.
3: Transfer weight on RF
4 1: LF to side
2: RF closes to LF
This is RF Alternative Basic Movement.
3: Transfer weight to LF
4 1: RF to side
Follower
2: RF closes to LF
This is LF Alternative Basic Movement.
Lady could do normal basic action, allowing body to move back more (pushing LF, try not to think about pulling center or foot back), allow R arm to extend.
Still need to have forward energy toward partner.
Create more separation from partner.
3: Transfer weight on LF
4 1: RF to side
2: LF closes to RF
This is RF Alternative Basic Movement.
3: Transfer weight to RF
4 1: LF to side
Note:
Silver & Gold:
may dance this figure as: (may start with the other foot).
count 2: close LF to RF without weight but with pressure. slight hip action
count 3: hold position with weight on RF. Footwork: flat. Slight hip action.
count 4 1: then LF side.
Man may dance LF Alternative Basic in place of first 3 steps of any figure commenced in Open Position or Fan position, while leading Lady to dance her normal first 3 steps of figure selected.
When commenced in Fan position, step 3 will be very small to keep correct positon in relation to Lady.
When Man dances a LF Alternative Basic from Open Position as precede to Natural Top, he turns slightly R on step 3.
Lady will dance "close fwd fwd" (RF LF RF)
